secondary_meaning: a building containing books and other resources
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Often used with 'à la bibliothèque' (at the library). Plural: bibliothèques
common_collocations: aller à la bibliothèque, la bibliothèque ferme, carte de bibliothèque
image_context: A grand library interior with tall bookshelves and reading tables
example_1: Je vais à la bibliothèque pour étudier.
example_1_translation: I go to the library to study.
example_2: La bibliothèque ferme à dix-huit heures.
example_2_translation: The library closes at six PM.
example_3: Elle emprunte des livres à la bibliothèque chaque semaine.
example_3_translation: She borrows books from the library every week.
pronunciation_tips: Stress first syllable: bib-li-o-THÈK. Roll 'r' sound lightly in second syllable.
memory_hook: Biblio (books) + thèque (library) - a place full of books.
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'bibliographe' (librarian by specialty). Gender: always 'la bibliothèque', not 'le'.

secondary_meaning: a building for displaying art and historical objects
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Common in tourist contexts. Plural: musées
common_collocations: aller au musée, le musée d'art, visiter un musée, musée gratuit
image_context: A museum building with classical architecture and columns
example_1: Le musée d'art est ouvert aujourd'hui.
example_1_translation: The art museum is open today.
example_2: J'ai visité le musée avec mes amis.
example_2_translation: I visited the museum with my friends.
example_3: L'entrée du musée est gratuite le dimanche.
example_3_translation: The museum entrance is free on Sunday.
pronunciation_tips: Rhymes with 'été'. Pronunciation: myu-ZAY. Final 'e' is silent.
memory_hook: Museum sounds similar in French - a place of cultural significance.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le musée', not 'la'. Avoid 'museau' (muzzle) - different word.

usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Often preceded by 'à la'. Plural: gares
common_collocations: aller à la gare, la gare centrale, gare de train
image_context: A busy train station platform with trains and travelers
example_1: Le train part de la gare centrale.
example_1_translation: The train leaves from the central station.
example_2: J'attends ma sœur à la gare.
example_2_translation: I am waiting for my sister at the train station.
example_3: La gare est près du centre-ville.
example_3_translation: The station is near the city center.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'gar'. Pronounced GAR. The 'r' is guttural (from back of throat).
memory_hook: Gare = where trains are guarded/parked. Remember feminine: 'la gare'.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'la gare' (feminine), not 'le gare'. Not 'garage' (car parking).

usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Short for 'métropolitain'. Common in Paris. Plural: métros
common_collocations: prendre le métro, ligne de métro, station de métro
image_context: A modern subway train arriving at an underground station platform
example_1: Je prends le métro pour aller au travail.
example_1_translation: I take the subway to go to work.
example_2: Le métro est plus rapide que l'autobus.
example_2_translation: The subway is faster than the bus.
example_3: La station de métro est fermée le dimanche.
example_3_translation: The metro station is closed on Sunday.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced MET-roh. Stress on first syllable. Short 'e' sound.
memory_hook: Métro = short for 'métropolitain' (metropolitan). Think Paris metro system.

secondary_meaning: a voucher for travel or entry
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Essential for transport. Plural: billets
common_collocations: acheter un billet, billet de train, billet d'avion, billet de bus
image_context: A train ticket with date, destination and seat number
example_1: J'ai acheté mon billet en ligne.
example_2: Montre ton billet, s'il te plaît.
example_3: Un billet de train coûte cinquante euros.
example_3_translation: A train ticket costs fifty euros.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced bee-YEH. Stress on second syllable. Similar to English 'bill' but more French.
memory_hook: Billet = small bill (like a small note). Bill and ticket are related concepts.

secondary_meaning: a container for carrying clothes and belongings while traveling
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Often plural in context. Plural: valises
common_collocations: faire sa valise, défaire sa valise, valise lourde
image_context: A vintage brown leather suitcase with straps on a hotel bed
example_2: Nous avons fait nos valises pour les vacances.
example_2_translation: We packed our suitcases for vacation.
example_3: Elle met ses vêtements dans sa valise.
example_3_translation: She puts her clothes in her suitcase.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced vah-LEEZ. Stress on second syllable. Final 'e' is silent.
memory_hook: Valise sounds like 'valid' - need a valid suitcase for travel.

secondary_meaning: the act or sport of swimming
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Usually uncountable. Plural: natations (rare)
common_collocations: faire de la natation, leçon de natation, champion de natation
image_context: A person swimming laps in a crystal clear swimming pool
example_1: La natation est mon sport préféré.
example_2: Je fais de la natation deux fois par semaine.
example_2_translation: I go swimming twice a week.
example_3: Les leçons de natation commencent en juin.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced nah-tah-see-OHN. Nasal 'on' at end. Stress on last syllable.
memory_hook: Natation = from Latin 'natare' (to swim). Nature of swimming.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'la natation' (feminine). Often used with 'faire de la' (to do/engage in).

secondary_meaning: a photograph or the art of taking photographs
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'la'. Can be countable (photos) or uncountable (hobby). Plural: photographies
common_collocations: faire de la photographie, appareil photo, photographie de nature
image_context: A professional camera lens focused on a scenic landscape
example_1: La photographie est mon passe-temps favori.
example_2: Il adore faire de la photographie de nature.
example_3: Elle expose ses photographies au musée.
example_3_translation: She exhibits her photographs at the museum.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced foh-toh-grah-FEE. Stress on last syllable. Greek roots: photo (light) + graphie (writing).
common_mistakes: Gender: 'la photographie' (feminine). Short form 'photo' is also feminine.

secondary_meaning: a game or problem requiring solution
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. English loanword, common in French. Plural: puzzles
common_collocations: faire un puzzle, pièce de puzzle, puzzle difficile
image_context: Colorful puzzle pieces being assembled on a wooden table
example_1: J'ai fait un puzzle de mille pièces.
example_2: Le puzzle est sur la table du salon.
example_2_translation: The puzzle is on the living room table.
example_3: Elle adore résoudre les puzzles difficiles.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced PUZ-uhl. English loanword. Stress on first syllable.
memory_hook: Puzzle = English word adopted into French. A puzzling game!
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le puzzle' (masculine) in French, even though English 'puzzle' sounds neutral.

secondary_meaning: a dairy product made from milk
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. France is famous for cheese varieties. Plural: fromages
common_collocations: fromage français, plateau de fromage, morceau de fromage
image_context: A variety of French cheeses on a wooden cutting board
example_1: La France est célèbre pour son fromage.
example_1_translation: France is famous for its cheese.
example_2: Je préfère le fromage avec du pain.
example_3: Nous avons acheté trois types de fromage au marché.
example_3_translation: We bought three types of cheese at the market.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced froh-MAHJ. Stress on second syllable. 'g' sounds like 'zh'.
memory_hook: Fromage from Latin 'formagium' - it's aged (forme = form/mold).

secondary_meaning: a beverage made from squeezed oranges
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Compound noun. Usually uncountable.
common_collocations: verre de jus d'orange, jus d'orange frais, jus d'orange pressé
image_context: A glass of fresh orange juice with an orange slice on the rim
example_1: Je bois du jus d'orange au petit déjeuner.
example_1_translation: I drink orange juice at breakfast.
example_2: Un verre de jus d'orange frais, s'il vous plaît.
example_2_translation: A glass of fresh orange juice, please.
example_3: Le jus d'orange est bon pour la santé.
example_3_translation: Orange juice is good for health.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced zhoo doh-RAHNG. 'j' sounds like 'zh'. Final 'e' is silent in orange.
memory_hook: Jus = juice. D'orange = of orange. Simple compound.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'le jus' (masculine). 'Jus' can mean juice or gravy in cooking.

usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Feminine: 'la professeure' or 'la prof'. Plural: professeurs
common_collocations: professeur de maths, professeur d'anglais, bon professeur
image_context: A teacher standing at the whiteboard in a classroom setting
example_2: Ma professeure de maths est très patiente.
example_2_translation: My math teacher is very patient.
example_3: Les professeurs ont une réunion aujourd'hui.
example_3_translation: The teachers have a meeting today.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced proh-feh-SER. Stress on last syllable. Informal: 'prof'.
memory_hook: Professor = one who professes knowledge. Profession + suffix -eur.

secondary_meaning: a board for writing in classroom; also a painting
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'le'. Plural: 'tableaux'. Can mean 'picture' or 'board'.
common_collocations: tableau noir, tableau blanc, écrire au tableau
image_context: A classroom whiteboard covered with colorful markers and writing
example_1_translation: Write the answer on the board.
example_2: Le professeur a écrit la date au tableau.
example_2_translation: The teacher wrote the date on the board.
example_3: Regardez le tableau pour voir les formules.
example_3_translation: Look at the board to see the formulas.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced tah-BLO. Stress on second syllable. Rhymes with 'water' in English.
memory_hook: Tableau = picture/board. Think of a board as a large canvas.

secondary_meaning: an electronic machine for processing data
usage_notes: Uses masculine article 'l'' (before vowel). Plural: ordinateurs
common_collocations: ordinateur portable, écran d'ordinateur, allumer l'ordinateur
image_context: A modern laptop computer open on a desk with screen glowing
example_1_translation: I use the computer to work.
example_3: Elle a un nouvel ordinateur portable.
example_3_translation: She has a new laptop computer.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced or-dee-nah-TER. Stress on last syllable. 'r' is guttural.
memory_hook: Ordinateur = from Latin 'ordinare' (to order/organize). Orders information.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'l'ordinateur' (masculine). Elision with 'l'' because word starts with vowel.

secondary_meaning: a machine that produces printed copies on paper
usage_notes: Uses feminine article 'l'' (before vowel). Note: feminine gender. Plural: imprimantes
common_collocations: imprimante couleur, imprimante laser, encre d'imprimante
image_context: A modern computer printer with paper in the tray
example_3_translation: Put the paper in the printer.
pronunciation_tips: Pronounced aw-pree-MAHNT. Stress on last syllable. Nasal 'on' sound.
memory_hook: Imprimante = from 'imprimer' (to print). The machine that prints.
common_mistakes: Gender: 'l'imprimante' (feminine), not masculine. CEFR B1 level, more advanced.
